name: Japan Open
stakes: This week in Tokyo is a pivotal moment for Indian badminton, with PV Sindhu vying for a prestigious Super 750 title and the rest of the contingent aiming to build momentum and secure valuable ranking points.
summary:
India's campaign at the Japan Open has been highlighted by a resurgent PV Sindhu, who has powered her way into the Women's Singles final. After a dominant performance against Tokyo Olympics champion Chen Yufei, where she led 21-19, 15-10 before Chen retired hurt with a hamstring issue, Sindhu now faces local favourite Akane Yamaguchi. This run marks a significant return to form for the former world champion, showcasing her thumping smashes and delicate net play.

While Sindhu carries the flag into the final, India's top Men's Doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy & Chirag Shetty, the third seeds and recent Singapore Open champions, are yet to begin their challenge in the Round of 32. Other Indian hopefuls like Lakshya Sen, HS Prannoy, and the Women's Doubles pair of Treesa Jolly & Gayatri Gopichand will be looking to make their mark as the tournament progresses, aiming for deep runs in this crucial Super 750 event.
